round1: Well, Round 1 is over, and most of you are off to a brutal start. Stephanie managed a solid three correct picks, but Marc and Mom are barely clinging to relevance with a measly one each. The Sharks series proved to be the ultimate filter, exposing the 'experts' who thought St. Louis had a chance.
round2: Round 2 is done, and while everyone managed to correctly predict Dallas, only Marc and Jaclyn bothered to get the game counts right on more than one series. Meanwhile, Stephanie and Mom demonstrated a remarkable ability to pick against reality, proving once again that hope is not a strategy.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, and both series went a full seven games. Seems everyone figured out New Jersey, but Mom and Jaclyn, you two missed the Dallas train entirely. As for game counts, nobody here knows a thing about playoff hockey.
round4: The Devils mercifully ended the Stars' season in 6 games. Dad knew better than anyone, while Marc at least picked the right team. As for Mom, Jaclyn, and Stephanie, their Dallas picks were about as useful as a goalie with a sieve for a glove.
overall: Alright, the 2000 playoffs are officially in the books, with the New Jersey Devils proving that sometimes the most thrilling hockey is simply... winning. Derrick, our pool champion, clearly understood this, dominating the field with 53 points, correctly picking ten teams and six series, making the rest of you look like you were guessing based on jersey colors. Stephanie put up a respectable fight for second, managing 45 points and seven correct series, which is almost impressive compared to Jaclyn and Marc, who picked plenty of teams but apparently forgot to pick winners. As for Chrissy, with a pathetic 17 points and a single correct series, you've officially earned the "participation trophy" for showing up, though your picks suggest you were actually just participating in a different sport entirely. Better luck next decade, maybe.
